item.title: MOIL Ltd Gains 12.05%: 6 Key Factors Driving the Volatile Week
item.description: MOIL Ltd’s shares experienced significant volatility during the week ending 20 March 2026, ultimately closing with a strong gain of 12.05% despite a challenging market backdrop. The stock’s performance notably outpaced the Sensex, which declined marginally by 0.28% over the same period. This review analyses the key events and underlying factors that shaped MOIL’s price action, including sharp intraday swings, rating changes, valuation shifts, and sectoral dynamics. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: D B Corp Ltd Gains 2.94%: 2 Key Valuation and Price Moves This Week
item.description: D B Corp Ltd’s shares advanced by 2.94% over the week ending 20 March 2026, closing at Rs.206.60 compared to Rs.200.70 the previous Friday. This performance notably outpaced the Sensex, which declined marginally by 0.28% during the same period. The week was marked by a recovery from a 52-week low and improved valuation metrics, despite ongoing challenges in profitability and market sentiment. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Gujarat Alkalies & Chemicals Ltd Surges 25.01%: 7 Key Developments Shaping the Week
item.description: Gujarat Alkalies & Chemicals Ltd delivered a remarkable weekly performance, surging 25.01% from Rs.430.50 to Rs.538.15 between 16 and 20 March 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined marginally by 0.28% over the same period. This sharp rebound followed a 52-week low on 16 March and was supported by heavy trading volumes, strong institutional interest, and a series of technical and valuation shifts that shaped investor sentiment throughout the week. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Deccan Cements Declines 0.65%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Downtrend
item.description: Deccan Cements Ltd. closed the week ending 20 March 2026 at Rs.647.40, down 0.65% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.651.65. This modest decline came amid a broader market pullback, with the Sensex falling 0.28% over the same period to 33,423.61. The stock’s performance was marked by volatility and bearish technical signals, culminating in a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating by MarketsMOJO on 19 March. Investors faced a challenging week as deteriorating financial metrics and negative momentum weighed on sentiment. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Sylph Industries Ltd Falls 9.68%: 3 Key Technical Signals Driving the Week
item.description: Sylph Industries Ltd experienced a challenging week from 16 to 20 March 2026, with its share price declining by 9.68% to close at Rs.0.84, underperforming the Sensex which fell marginally by 0.28%. Despite hitting new 52-week highs early in the week, the stock faced profit-taking and volatility amid mixed market conditions and technical shifts, culminating in a notable weekly loss for the micro-cap software company. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Aksh Optifibre Gains 1.76%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Move
item.description: Aksh Optifibre Ltd experienced a volatile week, ultimately closing with a modest gain of 1.76% against a Sensex decline of 0.28%. The stock’s trajectory was marked by a sharp fall to a 52-week low on 16 March, followed by a dramatic rebound hitting the upper circuit on 17 March. These contrasting moves highlight a week of intense market activity and shifting investor sentiment. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Sasken Technologies Gains 16.33%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Rally
item.description: Sasken Technologies Ltd delivered a strong weekly performance, surging 16.33% from Rs.1,007.45 on 13 Mar 2026 to Rs.1,172.00 on 20 Mar 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined marginally by 0.28% over the same period. The week was marked by a sharp rebound from a 52-week low, robust intraday gains, and mixed technical signals, reflecting a complex but ultimately positive trading environment for the stock. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Steel Authority Of India Ltd Gains 3.77%: 3 Key Technical Shifts Shape the Week
item.description: Steel Authority Of India Ltd. (SAIL) closed the week ending 20 March 2026 with a solid gain of 3.77%, rising from Rs.149.90 to Rs.155.55. This performance notably outpaced the Sensex, which declined by 0.28% over the same period, underscoring SAIL’s relative strength amid a mixed market environment. The week was marked by significant intraday rallies, technical momentum shifts, and sector-wide dynamics that shaped the stock’s trajectory. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Power Finance Corporation Ltd Gains 1.86%: 6 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Momentum
item.description: Power Finance Corporation Ltd (PFC) delivered a modest weekly gain of 1.86%, closing at Rs.413.00 on 20 March 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.28% over the same period. The week was marked by a series of technical developments, intraday highs, and a notable upgrade in the stock’s rating, reflecting a cautiously optimistic market sentiment amid mixed technical signals and sectoral pressures. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Reliance Communications Ltd Falls 11.76%: Four Days of Circuit Limits and a Sharp Rebound
item.description: Reliance Communications Ltd experienced a turbulent week from 16 to 20 March 2026, with its stock price declining sharply by 11.76%, closing at Rs.0.90 from Rs.1.02 the previous Friday. This underperformance contrasted with the relatively stable Sensex, which fell marginally by 0.28% over the same period. The week was marked by consecutive lower circuit hits amid heavy selling pressure, culminating in a strong rebound on the final trading day that triggered the upper circuit limit. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: National Standard (India) Ltd Gains 0.91%: 4 Key Technical Shifts Shape the Week
item.description: National Standard (India) Ltd closed the week with a modest gain of 0.91%, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.28%. The stock exhibited mixed technical signals throughout the week, with shifts from strongly bearish to mildly bearish momentum and intermittent bullish indicators on weekly charts. Despite these technical nuances, the company’s financial challenges and valuation risks remain prominent, shaping a cautious outlook for investors. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Bajaj Finserv Declines 1.66%: 2 Key Events Mark a Challenging Week
item.description: Bajaj Finserv Ltd’s shares closed the week ending 20 March 2026 at Rs.1,709.75, down 1.66% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.1,738.60. This underperformance contrasted with the Sensex’s modest 0.28% decline over the same period, reflecting a challenging week marked by a sharp intraday drop to a 52-week low and persistent technical weakness amid broader market volatility. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Aryaman Financial Services Ltd Falls 3.90%: Valuation and Technical Shifts Shape Weekly Trend
item.description: Aryaman Financial Services Ltd experienced a challenging week, closing at Rs.598.95 on 20 March 2026, down 3.90% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.623.25. This decline outpaced the Sensex’s modest 0.28% fall over the same period, reflecting heightened valuation concerns and mixed technical momentum amid volatile trading and sector headwinds. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Vikas Ecotech Ltd Falls 3.82%: 2 Key Events Mark a Challenging Week
item.description: Vikas Ecotech Ltd’s shares declined by 3.82% over the week ending 20 March 2026, closing at Rs.1.26 from Rs.1.31 the previous Friday. This underperformance contrasted with the Sensex’s marginal 0.28% decline, reflecting company-specific challenges amid heavy selling pressure and a new 52-week low. The week was marked by sharp intraday falls, circuit breaker triggers, and persistent bearish technical signals. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Bank Of Baroda’s Volatile Week: -0.36% Price Change Amid Mixed Market Signals
item.description: Bank Of Baroda’s shares experienced a turbulent week ending 20 March 2026, closing marginally lower by 0.36% at Rs.280.10 compared to the previous Friday’s close of Rs.281.10. This performance slightly underperformed the Sensex, which declined 0.28% over the same period. The week was marked by sharp intraday swings, with the stock hitting an intraday low of Rs.274.5 on 19 March before rebounding strongly to an intraday high of Rs.282.5 on 20 March, reflecting mixed market sentiment and sectoral pressures. Time: 5 hours ago
